title: banksia integrifolia subsp. integrifolia
intro: Banksia integrifolia subsp. integrifolia, commonly known as the coastal banksia, is a subspecies of the Banksia integrifolia tree native to the southeastern regions of Australia. It is a tall, evergreen tree with distinctive white, yellow, or red flowers and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The coastal banksia has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its rich content of flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which provide a range of health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical and internal use.
